title: Connect Code
subtitle: Paste a GitHub repository URL to load and explore the code.

explorer.title: Explorer
explorer.empty:
Enter a GitHub URL
and press Load

url_input.retry_hint: or pick from the library below ↓
url_input.my_repos_connect_hint: Connect GitHub in your profile to pick a repo directly
url_input.invalid_url: Please enter a valid GitHub URL. (e.g. https://github.com/owner/repo)
url_input.truncated: ⚠️ Too many files — only some are shown.

body.empty: Select a file to view its contents

next_step.intro: Your design is done. Here are 3 ways to turn this spec into real code — writing the code itself happens through the methods below, not in Harness.

self.title: I'll build it myself
self.desc: You can use AI coding tools like Cursor or Claude Code
self.step2: Unpack it into a new Cursor / Claude Code project and paste the start prompt.
self.step3: Paste the resulting GitHub repository into the input below to review it.

outsource.title: I'll hand it to a developer
outsource.desc: An agency or team developer will write the code
outsource.step2: Hand it to a developer or agency as-is and it becomes a work order.
outsource.step3: Receive the finished repository and review/accept it in the input below.
outsource.soon: A work order template for non-developers (budget, schedule, feature scope) is coming soon.

have.title: I already have code
have.desc: Just need to review and accept it
have.body: Paste your GitHub repository URL into the input just below to explore the code and check spec compliance in the Lint step.

agent_export.title: Hand off to AI coding tools
agent_export.desc: We'll turn your design spec into files that Cursor and Claude Code can read directly.

includes.plan: BUILD_PLAN.md — the AI's “how to build it” summary (stack, order, acceptance criteria)
includes.prompt: START_PROMPT.txt — a first instruction you can paste to start right away

agent_export.no_project_warn: Select a project first and the spec will be included in the bundle.

toast.copy_ok: ✓ Start prompt copied — paste it into Cursor or Claude
toast.copy_fail: Copy was blocked — use START_PROMPT.txt inside the bundle
toast.bundling: Gathering specs and building the bundle...

bundle.spec_readme_empty: No specs yet. Complete Harness's Plan and Design steps first, and this folder will be filled with PRD · Architecture · DDD · SPACK.

work_rules.r1_source_of_truth: Treat the `spec/` documents as the **single source of truth**. Do not add features that are not in the spec.
work_rules.r2_incremental: Implement one feature at a time, incrementally, in actually-working units.
work_rules.r3_summarize_before: Before any large change, first summarize what you will change and how.
work_rules.r4_new_lib_consent: Before adopting a new library or framework, propose it with a reason and get consent first. (Prefer the stack defined in the spec.)
work_rules.r5_import_registry: For imports and dependencies, **verify the package actually exists in the official registry (pypi, npm, etc.)**. Do not invent functions or options by guessing.
work_rules.r6_undecided_stop: Items marked **⚠️ 미정** in the spec (request/response shapes, data attributes, owning service, etc.) mean the design is still empty. **Do not fill them in on your own — stop and ask the user.** Guessing is the most common cause of failure.
work_rules.r7_run_verify: Consider a feature done only after you have actually run it and confirmed it works. Judge by the run result, not by "it should work."
work_rules.r8_ask_when_ambiguous: Do not guess at ambiguous parts — ask which part of the spec is unclear.
work_rules.r9_assumptions_risks: Before starting a large task, first write down **2–3 assumptions, risks, and uncertainties each** for the plan.
work_rules.r10_small_commits: Keep commits small — one thing per commit.

role_line.with_stack: You are a senior developer fluent in **{stack}**. Follow the design spec below faithfully.
role_line.no_stack: You are a careful senior developer. Follow the design spec below faithfully.

context.reader_agents: AI coding tools such as Cursor · Codex · Windsurf
context.doc_status: - `IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.md` — what is already built (✅) / not built (⬜) ★ read this first
context.doc_build_plan: - `BUILD_PLAN.md` — how to build it (recommended stack · build order · acceptance criteria) ★ read first
context.doc_empty: - (No spec yet — complete Harness's Plan and Design first)
context.enhance_note:
> ⚠️ **This project was imported from an existing codebase (not an empty folder).**
> Do not rebuild from scratch — respect the ✅ (already built) items in `IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.md` and add or improve only the ⬜ (not built)
> items on top of the existing code. Verify even the ✅ items once against the real code (the mapping is auto-estimated).
context.autoload_note: This file is the context that {reader} reads automatically when opening the project.
context.gen_note: Based on the Harness design spec · Generated: {stamp}
context.specs_lead: Before writing any code, read the documents in the `spec/` folder first.
context.footer: 🤖 Generated by Harness — planning by Harness, coding by {coder}.

start.greenfield:
I want to build the "{name}" project. The design spec is in this project's spec/ folder and CLAUDE.md (I unpacked the Harness bundle into it).

1) First read all the documents in spec/, then summarize what we're building in 3–5 lines.
2) Propose which tech stack to use and briefly explain why. (If the spec already fixes a stack, prefer that.)
3) First write down 2–3 assumptions, risks, and uncertainties each for this plan.
4) Lay out a step-by-step build plan starting from the smallest working unit (MVP).
5) Once I confirm, start writing code from step 1.

Rules:
- Do not add features that are not in the spec. Do not guess at ambiguous parts — ask me.
- For items marked ⚠️ 미정 in the spec (request/response shapes, data attributes, owning service, etc.), do not fill them in on your own — stop and ask me.
- Verify that imports and libraries actually exist in the official registry before using them. Do not invent functions or options.
- Run each step and confirm it works before moving to the next.
start.enhance:
I want to **continue improving and extending the "{name}" project on top of existing, already-built code**, not build it from scratch.

This folder includes an `IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.md` that came with it — it lists which design-spec items are ✅ already built / ⬜ not yet built. The detailed design is in the `spec/` folder.

‼️ **This bundle assumes you are working inside the repository it was imported from.** The files for ✅ items actually exist in that repo.

1) First read `IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.md` and the existing code, then summarize what is currently implemented in 3–5 lines.
2) Make a step-by-step plan for which ⬜ (not built) items to add or improve this round. Respect the existing code for ✅ (already built) items and do not rewrite them carelessly.
3) First write down 2–3 assumptions, risks, and uncertainties each for this plan.
4) Once I confirm, implement the ⬜ items first, matching the existing code style.

Rules:
- **Do not start a new project from scratch.** Follow the existing code's structure, naming, and tech stack, and build on top of it.
- Check even the ✅ items against the real code once. If STATUS and the code differ, trust the code but let me know. (The matching is auto-estimated, so it's not 100%.)
- **STATUS reflects the last analysis point in time.** Compare the current PRD in `spec/` against STATUS; if an item exists in the PRD but is in neither the ✅ nor ⬜ lists of STATUS, treat it as ⬜ (not built) and add it.
- Do not carelessly delete or change existing code that is not in the spec (the drift in STATUS).
- Do not add features that are not in the spec. For ⚠️ 미정 items, stop and ask. Verify that imports and libraries actually exist.
- Run each step and confirm it works before moving to the next.

status.title: Implementation Status (IMPLEMENTATION STATUS)
status.lead1: This project was **imported from an existing codebase**. Below is the auto-mapped status of "design spec ↔ actual code."
status.lead2: ‼️ Work **inside the repository this bundle was imported from**. The files for ✅ items are in that repo.
status.lead3: Generated: {stamp} · The mapping is auto-estimated, so it's not 100% — verify even the ✅ items against the real code once.
status.lead4: ⚠️ This status reflects the **last analysis point in time**. If you have since edited the spec (meeting notes / PRD), re-run analyzeLineage (or Lint) and regenerate the bundle. Until then, check whether the current PRD in `spec/` has items not in the lists below, and if so treat them as not built (⬜).
status.sec_done: ✅ Already built — do not rewrite (verify and refine only)
status.sec_missing: ⬜ Not built yet — add this round
status.sec_weak: ⚠️ Partial / low-confidence — verify against the real code
status.sec_drift: 🔎 Existing code not in the spec (drift) — do not change carelessly
status.empty_missing: none — add new requirements and re-analyze to fill this in
status.footer: 🤖 Generated by Harness — build on top of the existing implementation (do not start over from scratch).
